title: BenchmarkForged payload
description: Emitted by forge(schema_name='bench', ...) on every successful create — both fresh creates and idempotent re-runs (existing slug returned). Records that a benchmark harness landed in bench_harnesses. Consumers: dashboard bench-harnesses page (future), measure.bench_run (looks up the row), and the chain-close-completeness audit which checks that every chain that ships a perf-touch task also registers a bench.

slug.description: The kebab/snake-case bench identifier (e.g. `parse-context`). Unique within the project_id scope.

binary_path.description: Repo-relative path to the harness binary or `go run` target.

baseline_json_path.description: Repo-relative path to the baseline JSON file.

parse_output_as.description: How bench_run parses the harness's stdout. v1 ships json only.

timeout_ms.description: Subprocess wall-clock budget. Optional on the wire; defaults to 60000.

idempotent.description: True when the call was an idempotent re-run (existing slug returned without writing). False on fresh creates. Optional; treat absent as false.

flag_set_json.description: Canonical normalized JSON array of {flag,value} pairs (forge.NormalizeFlagSet output) — the flag_set_json column the harness re-tokenizes at run time. OPTIONAL and intentionally NOT minLength-constrained: the two pre-P2-A (2026-05-29) BenchmarkForged events predate this field and must stay valid as the immutable baseline, while every new event populates it. The bench_harnesses fold reads it back; an absent/empty value marks a pre-bump event the fold skips.

gate_metrics.description: Comma-separated allowlist of deterministic metric-name patterns that constitute the regression gate (the gate_metrics column). Empty = report-only. Optional (added P2-A alongside flag_set_json so the fold can reconstruct the full bench_harnesses row).
